ip_outcome_note: Time Mage (Final Fantasy Tactics Advance) confirmed via card category field 'FFTA' per the FFTCG Crystarium Opus VIII review and the figure's rubber-limbed chibi design canonical to Ryoma Ito's FFTA character art style. Internal Square Enix IP — no external crossover risk. No IP callout block required.

trivia_bullets.text: Opus VIII (released March 2019) introduced Final Fantasy XIV: Stormblood and Final Fantasy XV as new titles to FFTCG, alongside returning FFTA content featuring exclusive original artwork by Ryoma Ito (the canonical FFTA character designer).
sources: Square Enix FF Portal: Opus VIII announcement via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: IP verified: Time Mage (FFTA) confirmed via card category 'FFTA'. In Final Fantasy Tactics Advance, the Time Mage job is accessible only to Nu Mou and Moogle races — not humans — placing the depicted squat rubber-limbed figure within the Nu Mou or Moogle race job design canon.
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki: Time Mage (Tactics Advance) via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: 8-039C is an Ice Forward card, cost 3, 7,000 power, Standard Unit, category FFTA. Ability: 'When Time Mage enters the field, you may discard 1 card from your hand. If you do so, dull and Freeze all the Forwards of the same cost as the discarded card opponent controls.'
sources: FFTCG Crystarium: How to Win Limited — Opus VIII Review (exact card text)

trivia_bullets.text: Community sentiment on FFTCG Crystarium (rated 3/5 in Opus VIII Limited review): 'Attaching a massively discounted O1 Shiva to an on-curve body, Time Mage is going to clear the way for you to clock your opponent for tons of damage, or give you a ton of breathing room to restabilize... Time Mage will end games in a hurry.' Considered strong in draft, mid-tier in constructed.
sources: FFTCG Crystarium: How to Win Limited — Opus VIII Review
